design_philosophy.core_loop: Engage in aerial dogfighting: target enemies with cannon/missiles, manage time by completing stage objectives, advance through progressively challenging combat scenarios
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure vs. combat completion—player must destroy objectives before timer expires while managing incoming fire from multiple directions (front and back)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot an experimental fighter jet in high-stakes aerial combat missions; progress from basic A8M5 to enhanced A8M6 airframe
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid perspective system that dynamically shifts from first-person flight to third-person evasion when missile-locked; player agency in target selection (ships, fighters, tanks) within scripted mission structure; arcade-rooted design emphasizing skill execution over narrative

difficulty.continues_system: Time-based progression—player earns additional time and stage advancement by completing mission objectives; running out of time triggers game over
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing tied to objective completion
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players choose which targets to prioritize within each stage (selective targeting mechanic); some home ports feature upgrade progression via earned points to customize difficulty curve

progression.upgrade_system: Aircraft platform upgrades (A8M5→A8M6 across campaign); Game Gear port features point-based jet upgrades for customization
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression gated by mission objective completion and time accumulation; stage advancement requires destroying designated targets
replayability_hooks: Multiple target types per stage encourage varied combat approaches
R360 arcade cabinet motion feedback creates distinct experience from standup/sit-down versions
Home ports introduce features absent from arcade (Master System bosses, Game Gear upgrades, Mega Drive perspective switching)
Leaderboard-driven arcade scoring incentivizes high-score runs

world_building_techniques: Enemy variety (war balloons, bombers, ace fighter pilot) establishes combat hierarchy
Aircraft platform naming (A8M5/A8M6) suggests military-industrial progression
Title premise ('G-force induced Loss Of Consciousness') provides context flavor without explicit narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Escalating mechanical challenges: early bosses (War Balloon, Bomber) represent environmental/vehicle threats; final boss mirrors player's aircraft with enhanced durability and aggression, forcing pattern adaptation
notable_bosses: Enemy Ace (final boss)—uses identical plane as player with superior stats, requiring learned evasion and targeting precision

faction_indicators: Enemy forces (unnamed antagonist nation) vs. player's military affiliation (experimental aircraft test pilot role)
danger_signals: Missile lock triggering perspective shift to third-person—visual signal of imminent danger requiring evasive maneuvers
Incoming fire from front and back simultaneously creates multi-directional threat awareness
Time pressure mechanic generates tension escalation as objectives remain incomplete
environmental_storytelling: Diverse target palette (ships, tanks, aircraft) suggests military engagement across multiple domains
Boss progression (stationary balloon → mobile bomber → agile ace) reflects escalating enemy sophistication
First-to-third-person perspective shift narrativizes loss of control, reinforcing vulnerability states
